verdict_reasoning: FN1 (Fibronectin 1) is a secreted glycoprotein with no transmembrane domain, no GPI anchor, and no outer-leaflet lipidation. It exists as either soluble plasma fibronectin (hepatocyte-secreted dimeric form) or cellular fibronectin deposited as insoluble fibrils in the ECM. During matrix assembly, soluble FN dimers transiently bind α5β1 integrin receptors on the cell surface — but the integrin is the TM-anchored recruiter; FN itself has no membrane anchor and is in equilibrium with the soluble pool during this stage. The ECM covalent cross-linking (by transglutaminases) deposits FN into the matrix stroma, not onto a TM-anchored scaffold — matrix deposition is explicitly excluded from stable_surface_attachment. Pre-no checklist: no gene-specific evidence of cancer/apoptosis/activation outer-leaflet ecto-display; no narrow germline/developmental surface pool with its own anchor; not a lysosomal TM protein; the PM-adjacent pool during fibrillogenesis is the recruiter (integrin) model, not a minority dual-localization PM pool with FN's own anchor; no wash-resistant covalent tethering to a TM partner. FN1 is secreted_only.
